codeReview.summary: The commit implements relay URL validation, duplicate detection, Settings Add/Remove/Edit wiring, IndexedDB persistence via AppStateValue.updateRelays, and RuntimeRelayPump hot-reload behavior with targeted unit/component coverage. However, the feature contract explicitly includes VAL-CROSS-005 relay-churn mid-sign behavior and calls for a multi-device spec, which is missing from this commit.

issues.description: Required multi-device coverage for VAL-CROSS-005 was not delivered in this feature commit. features.json lists "Multi-device e2e: src/e2e/multi-device/relay-churn.spec.ts" under verificationSteps (m5-relay-list-persist), but this file does not exist in commit e95ade9 (`git show e95ade9:src/e2e/multi-device/relay-churn.spec.ts` fails). The handoff also explicitly states this work was left undone.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker skill's skip exception for "pure ... data-wiring fixes" appears too broad in practice; it was used to skip agent-browser even though this feature added/changed user interactions (relay Add/Remove/Edit controls, inline validation and edit states). Consider tightening the exception wording to explicitly exclude interaction-flow changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with deviation "agent-browser visual verification"; commit adds interactive relay CRUD UI in src/screens/DashboardScreen/sidebar/SettingsSidebar.tsx

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md and ui-screen-worker both require RED-before-GREEN evidence, but the handoff verification block only shows passing runs. A stronger shared-state/process guard (e.g., explicit required red-run field in handoff schema) would make adherence auditable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md "TDD required" and ui-screen-worker step 4/6 require failing test evidence; handoff.verification.commandsRun includes only successful tsc/vitest executions.

summary: Review failed: core relay-list persistence/hot-reload implementation is substantial, but the feature did not include the required VAL-CROSS-005 multi-device relay-churn spec in this commit, leaving a stated acceptance slice uncovered.
